qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: 2FA bypass via personal access token creation API
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns 2FA enforcement on PAT creation APIs, not protected branch deletion in DeleteBranchPost or the missing protected/default-branch checks described by the advisory.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Mirror settings bypass local-path and local-network import restrictions
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns mirror-origin rewriting and mirror sync importing attacker-selected local repositories, not the branch deletion backend path or missing protected-branch authorization checks from the advisory.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Repository upload flow writes through in-repo parent symlinks
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns the upload editor following parent symlinks during file writes, not DeleteBranchPost or a missing protected/default-branch deletion guard.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable backend branch-deletion path in internal/route/repo/branch.go::DeleteBranchPost or the missing protected/default-branch checks that permit protected branch deletion by a writer.
evidence: The advisory describes a web-interface protected-branch deletion bypass in internal/route/repo/branch.go::DeleteBranchPost, mounted at the branch deletion POST route and missing protected-branch and default-branch checks after reqRepoWriter.

auth_2fa_pat_bypass instead targets API basic-auth handling for POST/GET /api/v1/users/:username/tokens, with code locations in internal/context/auth.go, internal/route/api/v1/api.go, internal/route/api/v1/user/access_tokens.go, and internal/database/access_tokens.go.
mirror_local_path_bypass instead targets mirror settings and sync flows in internal/route/repo/setting.go and internal/database/mirror.go, where mirror origin updates bypass local-path restrictions.

upload_symlink_arbitrary_write instead targets upload/editor write handling in internal/route/repo/editor.go, internal/database/repo_editor.go, and internal/pathutil/pathutil.go, where parent symlinks are followed during file creation.
